column5:
As a video blogger, I've had my fair share of encounters with trolling incidents, but one that stands out happened after I made a video discussing gender inequality. In the video, I shared my perspective on certain societal norms and how they contribute to disparities between men and women in certain aspects of life.
Shortly after the video was posted, I started receiving a barrage of hateful comments and messages across my social media platforms. Some individuals accused me of being sexist or misogynistic, while others resorted to personal attacks and derogatory language. They criticized my viewpoints without engaging in constructive dialogue or offering alternative perspectives.
column8: Being trolled can evoke a range of emotions, from frustration and annoyance to hurt and disappointment. Initially, it can feel disheartening to see hateful or derogatory comments directed at you, especially when they attack your character or beliefs. It's natural to feel defensive or upset in the face of such negativity.
column9: Block the troll;Report the trolling behaviour;Seek support from others
column12: Being trolled can sometimes spill over into real life, affecting my mood and mental well-being, especially if the trolling becomes particularly intense or persistent. It can be challenging to shake off the negative energy and emotions brought on by hateful comments or personal attacks, even when I try to compartmentalize my online presence from my offline life.

column5:
Last year, during a class discussion about a controversial topic, I shared my opinion, which was different from the majority of my classmates. After class, I received a flood of messages on social media from some of my classmates, mocking me for my views and calling me names. They weren't interested in having a respectful debate; instead, they just wanted to ridicule me for thinking differently.

One particular incident that stood out was when someone created a fake account impersonating me and posted offensive comments on various social media platforms. They used my name and profile picture to make it seem like I was saying hurtful things, which caused confusion and outrage among my peers.

It was incredibly frustrating and hurtful to see my words twisted and manipulated in such a way. I felt betrayed by the lack of empathy and understanding from my classmates and helpless in the face of this trolling attack. It made me realize how toxic online behavior can spill over into real life and affect people's perceptions of you.
column8: At first, I might feel shocked or caught off guard by the trolling, especially if it's unexpected or particularly harsh. It can be difficult to understand why someone would go out of their way to be hurtful or disrespectful.
column9: Ignore it;Block the troll
column12: In extreme cases, trolling can escalate to harassment or threats, which can make me feel unsafe both online and in real life. It's unsettling to know that there are people out there who are willing to go to such lengths to intimidate or harm me.

column5: Certainly. One specific example of a trolling incident I encountered while teaching online was during a live class session. A student repeatedly interrupted the lesson by posting irrelevant or provocative comments in the chat box. Despite reminders to stay focused on the lesson material, the student continued to disrupt the class with disruptive behavior, making it challenging for other students to engage and participate effectively.
column8:
When I'm trolled while teaching online, I feel a mix of frustration and disappointment. It's frustrating because trolling disrupts the learning environment and detracts from the educational experience for all students involved. As an educator, I put effort into creating a positive and productive learning environment, so encountering trolling behavior undermines those efforts.

Additionally, I may feel disappointed that a student would choose to engage in trolling behavior rather than actively participate in the class and benefit from the learning opportunities provided. It's disheartening to see someone prioritize disruption over learning and respect for their peers and instructor.

column12: In rare cases, trolling incidents may escalate to harassment or threats, which can raise concerns about personal safety. While such instances are uncommon, they can still create feelings of unease or anxiety, both during and after the trolling incident.
